intro: Fissistigma schlechteri is a tropical plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family, commonly known as the custard apple or sugar apple family. The plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Fissistigma schlechteri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, cytotoxic, and immunomodulatory activities. In traditional medicine, Fissistigma schlechteri is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and it is also used in various herbal formulations, such as capsules and infusions, to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various diseases.
